sessions.intent: Respond to test-designer review of PLang test module plan, produce revised v2 plan

before.assumptions: v1 plan exists with test module design. Test-designer has reviewed and provided 11 feedback points covering scope gaps, corrections, and deferrals.
before.risk: Plan-only session. No code changes. Low risk.

sessions.plan: Read test-designer review, verify claims against codebase (DefaultAssertProvider, existing test conventions, Action lifecycle events), write v2 plan addressing all 11 points with accept/correct/defer decisions.

actions.context: Structured summary of test-designer review points
Revised plan addressing all 11 review points with rationale

after.status: v2 plan complete, awaiting user approval before implementation
after.notes: All 11 review points addressed. Key corrections: App isolation boundary, variable dump timing, AfterAction events for coverage.

sessions.intent: Architect synthesis: merge runtime2 into branch, read tester v2 + test-designer review, produce v1 architect plan with scope decisions locked after discussion with Ingi

before.assumptions: Branch was 173 commits behind runtime2. Tester v1+v2 plans and test-designer review exist but predate recent runtime2 changes (Runtime2→App rename, modifiers, events). Failing PLang tests motivate the need for this tool.
before.risk: Plan-only. No runtime code changes in this session. Merge from runtime2 is conflict-free because all branch changes are under .bot/.

sessions.plan:
Scope locked in discussion 2026-04-17:

IN SCOPE (v1):
1. Testing class upgrade to runner — owns Results, Coverage, Config; configured via --test={timeout,parallel,include,exclude,verbose}.
2. test.discover action — scan *.test.goal, load .pr, freshness check (hash), extract user tags via test.tag scan + auto-tags via [RequiresCapability] attribute reflection.
3. test.tag action — user-declared tags, no-op at runtime, metadata at discovery.
4. test.run action — C# main loop. Fresh App.@this per file (file-boundary = App-boundary, sub-goals and external calls share). Parallel execution (default Environment.ProcessorCount). 30s default timeout via CancellationTokenSource. Subscribes AfterAction for coverage.
5. test.report action — console + JSON + JUnit XML + two coverage tables (module.action + branch).
6. [RequiresCapability(params string[])] attribute on action handlers. http.* gets 'network', llm.ask gets 'llm'.
7. Variables.Snapshot() API + AssertionError.Variables field. Assert handlers capture scope-chain snapshot on failure.
8. AfterAction event payload widened to (Action, Data). Surgical change, all call sites updated same commit. Unlocks module.action coverage + branch tracking without touching Data itself.
9. condition.if publishes Properties['branch_index'] in its result Data. Enables branch coverage for if/elseif/else.
10. system/test.goal rewritten — no foreach. Single test.run call with discovered list.
11. Per-test metadata in output: builder version + .pr hash for drift correlation.

DEFERRED: mutation testing (needs own design pass), conditional skip/precondition (tags cover static case, genuine dynamic cases rare), .golden.pr drift detection (builder concern), tag negation (v1 additive only), action-level capability overrides (not needed pre-emptively).

KEY DISCUSSION DECISIONS:
- Dropped test.dependency (tests can call each other for setup — isolation violated by ordered deps).
- Dropped test.skip (tags cover environmental preconditions, known-broken shouldn't be silenced).
- App isolation = file boundary, not goal boundary.
- Rejected Data.Action property in favor of widening AfterAction event payload — smaller blast radius, branch coverage falls out cleanly from (Action, Data) at fire site.
- [RequiresCapability] on action, not module (action-level for flexibility, params string[] for multi-capability actions).

Next step: hand off to test-designer for test-suite design.

Full plan at .bot/runtime2-test-module/architect/v1/plan.md.

actions.context: Architect v1 plan — synthesis of tester v2 + test-designer review + live discussion with Ingi. Covers scope, architecture, file layout, per-action detail, risks, handoff.
Merged origin/runtime2 into runtime2-test-module (173 commits). Zero conflicts — branch only had .bot/ changes. Pushed.

after.status: v1 plan written and approved by Ingi. Ready to hand off to test-designer.
after.notes: Plan-only session. No runtime code changed. Key decisions: AfterAction event payload widening (not Data.Action), [RequiresCapability] on handler, file-boundary isolation, branch_index in condition.if result.

sessions.intent: Design test suites that prove the PLang test module v1 works, per architect's approved plan

before.assumptions: Architect v1 plan approved. Locked scope: Testing class upgrade, test.discover/tag/run/report, [RequiresCapability], Variables.Snapshot, AssertionError.Variables, AfterAction payload widening, condition.if branch_index, system/test.goal rewrite. Existing PLang test-designer review (test-module-plan-review.md) predates the architect plan.
before.risk: Test design only — no production code. Risk is missing coverage areas or translating architect plan 1:1 without independent edge-case thinking (memory feedback_think_independently).

sessions.plan: 14 batches, ~109 tests (90 C# / 19 PLang). Foundation (batches 1–7): Testing class + Config, Results + Coverage, [RequiresCapability] attribute, Variables.Snapshot(), AssertionError.Variables, AfterAction payload widening, condition.if branch_index. Actions (8–11): test.discover, test.tag, test.run, test.report. Integration (12): system/test.goal end-to-end. Metadata (13): builder version + .pr hash. Safety net (14): independent edge cases and security tests — recursive test.run, path traversal, XML escaping in JUnit, ANSI injection via output.write, static-cache leakage across fresh Apps, --test={parallel:0|timeout:0}, Icelandic test.goal discovery. C# files under PLang.Tests/App/Test/; PLang files under Tests/TestModule/ (one goal per file per memory rule). Bodies are Assert.Fail('Not implemented') / throw 'not implemented'. Five open questions in plan.md section 9 (Results file split, CurrentTest thread-safety, JUnit XML variant, builder version source, capability auto-tag scope). Plan waiting for Ingi approval before writing batch 1.

actions.context: Test plan with 14 batches, C#/PLang split, independent edge cases, five open questions resolved with Ingi.

14 C# test files, 93 [Test] method signatures total, all bodies Assert.Fail('Not implemented'). One-line intent comment per test defines the spec. Namespace PLang.Tests.App.Testing (renamed from .Test to avoid shadowing TUnit's [Test]). Compiles clean.

19 PLang .test.goal files, one goal per file (memory feedback: multi-goal files overwritten by builder). Each has a comment block explaining the intent. Body is 'throw not implemented' — coder fills in with real assertions.

after.status: Test suite complete — 112 tests (93 C# + 19 PLang) across 14 batches, all compile clean. Ready for coder handoff.
after.notes: Every batch reviewed with Ingi before writing. Key design choices locked: flat config on Testing, Results/Coverage split, no AfterAction back-compat, .test/ output per-goal-dir, format=json default single-select, Goal.Hash for freshness, auto-tags traverse sub-goals, test.tag no-op outside tests. All tests carry a comment defining the spec; bodies are Assert.Fail/throw.
